revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster]][[ja:石清尾山古墳群]]
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster | native_name = 石清尾山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =; File:猫塚古墳（双方中円墳）.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|19|39.6|N|134|01|25.0|E|region:JP-37|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|34.32766667|N|134.02361111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|qid=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu|1=Takamatsu}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Takamatsu|qid=Q200707|1=Takamatsu}}| region = [[Shikoku]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles =; {{ill|Q11410445|qid=Q11410445|1=Sōhō-chūen Kofun}}| architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|19|39.6|N|134|01|25.0|E}}}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster'''|石清尾山古墳群| Iwaseoyama kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located on the Iwaseo mountains spreading across the Mineyama, Muromachi, Miyawaki, Nishikasuga, Tsuruichi, and Nishitakara neighborhoods of the city of {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|qid=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu|1=Takamatsu}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}} on the island of [[Shikoku]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The cluster was designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1934, with the era under protection expanded in 1985 and 1989..<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/2dd250|title=石清尾山古墳群|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 May 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Iwaseoyama Kofun cluster is located along the ridge line of the summit of Mt. Iwashio, (elevation 230 meters above sea level), which is located southwest of the center of Takamatsu city. The cluster consists of more than 200 piled stone mounds along with a number of embankment mounds. All were constructed over a period of approximately 100 years from the 4th to 5th centuries, or the early to late Kofun period. The piled stone mounds are rare in Japan, and are made from {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|qid=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}}, which forms the base of the summit area of the mountain. Of these tumuli, a total of 16 are designated the national historic site, consisting of 14 piled-stone mounds and two embanked Kofuns. The shape of the tumuli are diverse. Of the piled-stone tumuli, the Nekozuka Kofun, Kagamizuka Kofun, and Inariyama-hokutan Kofun are hour-glass shaped {{nihongo|''futa-hocho-enpun''|双方中円墳||}}-style tumuli, the Kita Otsuka East mound is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun||}}-style square tumulus, the Inariyama Minamitsuka North Kofun is a round {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style tumulus, and the others are keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} mounds. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
At 96 meters long and 5 meters high, the Nekozuka Kofun is the largest of the Kofuns. It was robbed in antiquity, but some surviving {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} include bronze mirrors and swords, iron swords, arrowheds and axes, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery.
In 1934, the Ishifunezuka Kofun was designated as a national historic site, but in 1985, a large number of ''kofun'' on the summit were additionally designated, and the historic site designation was renamed. In 1989, Tsuruo Shrine No. 4 Kofun was added to the designation. The ''kofun'' are maintained as a hiking course, and are located approximately 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Takamatsu Station (Kagawa)|en|Takamatsu Station (Kagawa)|qid=Q860503|lt=Takamatsu Station|1=Takamatsu Station (Kagawa)}} on the [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] {{ill|Yosan Line|en|Yosan Line|qid=Q575016|lt=Yosan Line|1=Yosan Line}}. . <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|qid=Q6573268|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [http://www.city.takamatsu.kagawa.jp/kurashi/kosodate/bunka/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shiseki/kofun2.html Takamatsu City Official site] {{in lang|ja}}
